cot_prefix: Okay, I need to figure out why the novelist can expect the publisher to print the manuscript exactly as written. Let me go through the provided text again to make sure I understand the key points. The passage says that novelists have absolute control over their text until it's published. It mentions that even a well-established novelist might deliver a manuscript and expect it to be printed as is. The reason given is that the novel is a single-channel medium (writing) and the writer's only restrictions are artistic. In contrast, plays and screenplays require collaboration because they involve multiple channels (actors, sets, etc.), so the original writer doesn't have the same control once production starts.
